an9.63:1.1: “Monks, these five are things that weaken the training.
an9.63:1.3: The taking of life, stealing, sexual misconduct, the telling of lies, and distilled & fermented beverages that are a cause for heedlessness.
an9.63:1.4: These five are things that weaken the training.
an9.63:2.1: “To abandon these five things that weaken the training, one should develop the four establishings of mindfulness.
an9.63:2.3: There is the case where a monk remains focused on the body in & of itself…
an9.63:2.6: mental qualities in & of themselves—ardent, alert, & mindful—subduing greed & distress with reference to the world.
an9.63:2.7: To abandon the five things that weaken the training, one should develop these four establishings of mindfulness.”
